key_insight: Topology optimization can tailor plasmonic metasurfaces to maximize attractive optical trapping forces on nanoparticles, with designs changing according to particle size and material.
engineering_relevance: The framework enables selective, potentially manufacturable nanoparticle trapping for applications such as biosensing, microfabrication, and quantum system assembly.
method: Density-based topology optimization of plasmonic metasurfaces
Maxwell stress tensor used to compute optical forces on nanoparticles
Free-form designs followed by manufacturability constraints for planar layouts
evidence_signal: The abstract reports optimized designs evaluated for different nanoparticle sizes and materials, showing higher trapping stiffness for smaller nanoparticles.
watch_item: Performance depends on nanoparticle size, material, and normally incident monochromatic excitation, so practical robustness under varied operating conditions needs validation.
paper_title: Topology optimized plasmonic metasurfaces for optical trapping of nanoparticles
